reasoning: There is no credible reporting as of October 2025 that any US ally is anywhere close to possessing an independent nuclear weapon. The process of building a nuclear weapon takes several years of dedicated effort, testing, and material refinement, which would be detected long before completion due to IAEA monitors and international intelligence. An ally being handed a fully autonomous weapon by an existing nuclear power is historically unprecedented and highly antithetical to global non-proliferation norms. Since there are only two months remaining in 2025 and zero indication of a completed weapons program, the probability is practically zero. I am forecasting the minimal allowed value, 0.02.
batch_queries: Will a US ally get a nuke in 2025?
